info.title: WebTop Calendar
info.description: This is Calendar service API enpoint.

get.summary: List all calendar objects belonging to a specific calendar
get.description: List all CalendarObjects of specified Calendar.

post.description: Creates new CalendarObject into specified Calendar.

get.description: Returns changed CalendarObjects (added/modified/deleted) since the specified syncToken. If token is not provided, the initial sync configuration will be returned.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limits the number of returned results

get.description: Returns a list of available Calendars with enabled synchronization.

get.summary: List all messages for a specific folder
get.description: Returns sync informations for the specified Calendar.

post.description: Add new Event into specified Calendar.

get.description:
Returns a list of Calendars readable by the current user: this includes both personal and incoming shared categories.

Param *_filter* supports the following fields in RSQL condition:
id, createdAt, updatedAt, userId, builtIn, provider, name, description, color, easSync

Param *_select* supports the following fields:
provider, builtIn, name, description, color, easSync, defSensitivity, defShowAs, defReminder, remoteSyncFrequency, remoteSyncTimestamp, remoteSyncToken

post.description: Adds new category specifying the owning user ID. If no user ID is provided, the owner will be the current user.

get.description: Gets the specified Calendar given its ID.

put.description: Update the specified Calendar given its ID. You can choose to update the entire object or only a subset of data.

delete.description: Delete a Calendar given its ID.

get.description:
Returns a list of Events from specified Calendar.

Param *_filter* supports the following fields in RSQL query conditions:
id, createdAt, updatedAt, rowStatus, status, organizer, organizerId, title, location, description, timezone, allDay, start, end, visibility, transparency, tagId

Param *_select* supports the following fields:
publicUid, location, description, visibility, transparency, href, reminder

post.description: Adds new Event into given Calendar.

get.description:
Get a set of Events that have been added, deleted, or updated in a specified Calendar, starting from a precise instant identified by a given syncToken.

Param *_select* supports the following fields:
location, description, visibility, transparency, href, reminder

parameters.name: calendar_id
parameters.description: The Calendar ID where the event will be added into

post.description: Adds new Event into specified Calendar.

get.summary: List Event instances in a specific time-range.
get.description:
Returns a list of Event instances from specified calendars.

Param *_filter* supports the following fields in RSQL query conditions:
id, createdAt, updatedAt, rowStatus, status, organizer, organizerId, title, location, description, timezone, allDay, start, end, visibility, transparency, tagId

Param *_select* supports the following fields:
publicUid, location, description, visibility, transparency, href, reminder

parameters.name: calendar_ids
parameters.description: List (comma-separated) of Calendar IDs whose contacts should be included in the results.

parameters.name: rangeStart
parameters.description: The start date and time (inclusive) of the time range, represented in ISO 8601 format and UTC time.

parameters.name: rangeEnd
parameters.description: The end date and time (exclusive) of the time range, represented in ISO 8601 format and UTC time.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Specified wheather to sort results by date

get.summary: List dates in a specific time-range within Event instances.
get.description:
Returns a list of dates that contains any Event instances according to specified calendars.

Param *_filter* supports the following fields in RSQL query conditions:
id, createdAt, updatedAt, rowStatus, status, organizer, organizerId, title, location, description, timezone, allDay, start, end, visibility, transparency, tagId

parameters.name: calendar_ids
parameters.description: List (comma-separated) of Calendar IDs whose contacts should be included in the results.

parameters.name: rangeStart
parameters.description: The start date and time (inclusive) of the time range, represented in ISO 8601 format and UTC time.

parameters.name: rangeEnd
parameters.description: The end date and time (exclusive) of the time range, represented in ISO 8601 format and UTC time.

get.description:
Retrieve a specific event instance identified by its instance ID.

The event_instance_id uses the format {eventId}.{date} and determines what is returned:

- {eventId}.00000000 — targets a single event or the master event of a recurring series. Returns the event object with its recurrence rules, but does not expand the individual occurrences of the series.
- {eventId}.{YYYYMMDD} — targets a single occurrence or an exception. Returns the resolved instance for that date, merging the master event data with any exception overrides applicable to that occurrence.

Param *_select* supports the following fields:
displayName, title, firstName, lastName, nickname, mobile, pager1, pager2, email1, email2, email3, im1, im2, im3, workAddress, workPostalCode, workCity, workState, workCountry, workTelephone1, workTelephone2, workFax, homeAddress, homePostalCode, homeCity, homeState, homeCountry, homeTelephone1, homeTelephone2, homeFax, otherAddress, otherPostalCode, otherCity, otherState, otherCountry, taxCode, vatNumber, eInvoicingCode

put.description:
Update a specific Event instance identified by its instance ID.

The event_instance_id uses the format {eventId}.{date} and determines the scope of the update:
- {eventId}.00000000 — targets a single event or the master event of a recurring series. For single events, the update applies to the event itself. For master events, the update applies to all instances in the series, preserving any existing exceptions.
- {eventId}.{YYYYMMDD} — targets a single occurrence; the update applies only to that instance, which is promoted to an exception within the series.

When targeting a single occurrence, the optional modify_since boolean parameter extends the scope of the update:
- modify_since=false (default) — updates only the specified instance
- modify_since=true — splits the series at the specified date; all occurrences from that date onward inherit the update, while earlier occurrences remain unchanged

Note: modify_since is ignored for single events and master events.
You can update the entire event object or a subset of its fields.

delete.description:
Delete a specific Event instance identified by its instance ID.

The event_instance_id uses the format {eventId}.{date} and determines the scope of the deletion:
- {eventId}.00000000 — targets a single event or the master event of a recurring series. For single events, the event is permanently deleted. For master events, the entire series is deleted including all its instances and exceptions.
- {eventId}.{YYYYMMDD} — targets a single occurrence; the occurrence is removed from the series and added to the exclusion list of the master event, leaving the rest of the series unaffected.

When targeting a single occurrence, the optional modify_since boolean parameter extends the scope of the deletion:
- modify_since=false (default) — deletes only the specified instance
- modify_since=true — splits the series at the specified date; all occurrences from that date onward are deleted, while earlier occurrences remain unchanged. This operation truncates the master event's recurrence rule accordingly.

Note: modify_since is ignored for single events and master events.

put.description: Update the specified Event instance given its instance ID. This is the quick version of the update operation where you can specify fresh values for some main fields.

put.summary: Update the response to an Event invitation
put.description: Update the invitation response for the specified Event instance.

DavCalObject.description: Bean for carry calendar object's fields

updatedFields.description: Specifies which of above fields have been updated

DavCalendarUpdate.description: Bean for carry calendar's updateable fields

DavCalObjectNew.description: Bean for carry new calendar object's fields

DavCalObjectChanged.description: Bean for carry fields of calendar object that have been changed

DavCalObjectsChanges.description: Bean for carry calendar object collection changes

deflt.description: Specifies if marked as predefined folder

CalendarBase.title: CalendarBase
CalendarBase.description: Represent a Calendar object with base updateable fields.

provider.description: Specifies the data provider. Defaults to `local`.

builtIn.description: Specifies whether the calendar is built-in.

color.description: The color associated to the calendar.

defVisibility.description: Default visibility value for new events.

defTransparency.description: Default transparency value for new events.

defReminder.description: Default reminder value for new events.

remoteSyncFrequency.description: Sync frequency (in minutes) for remote calendars (webcal, caldav).

remoteSyncTimestamp.description: The last remote sync execution timestamp, in ISO 8601 format and UTC time.

remoteSyncToken.description: The sync-token returned by the last remote sync.

providerParams.description: Configuration params for non-local calendars in JSON form.

Calendar.title: Calendar

etag.description: The revision identifier that refers to the last modification.

itemsETag.description: The revision identifier that refers to the last modification of the items.

createdAt.description: Creation timestamp in ISO 8601 format and UTC time.

updatedAt.description: Modification timestamp in ISO 8601 format and UTC time.

Calendar.description: Represent a Calendar that contains Events.

CalendarsResult.title: CalendarsResult
CalendarsResult.description: Represents a response object for a collection of Calendars.

totalCount.description: Items total count, usually used for pagination.

EventBase.title: EventBase
EventBase.description: Represent an Event object with base updateable fields.

rowStatus.description: Status of the event row: DF=default, RO=read-only.

timezone.description: The timezone ID. (Formatted as an IANA Time Zone Database name, e.g. "Europe/Zurich".)

allDay.description: Set to true if the event lasts all day.

start.description: The (inclusive) start time of the event. For a recurring event, this is the start time of the first instance.

end.description: The (exclusive) end time of the event. For a recurring event, this is the end time of the first instance.

location.description: The location of the event. Optional.

descriptionType.description: Specifies the data format of the description.

visibility.description: Visibility of the event: PU=public, PV=private

transparency.description: Whether the event blocks time on the calendar: TP=transparent, OP=opaque.

EventEx.title: EventEx
EventEx.description: Represent an Event object with extended data.

Event.title: Event

etag.description: The revision identifier that refers to the last modification.

createdAt.description: Creation timestamp in ISO 8601 format and UTC time.

updatedAt.description: Modification timestamp in ISO 8601 format and UTC time.

Event.description: Represent an Event, a scheduled occurrence on a calendar, such as a meeting, holiday, or time block.

EventInstance.title: EventInstance

iid.description: The event's unique ID that identifies an instance.

instanceType.description: The event instance type. The possible values are: SI=single, MA=master, OC=occurrence, EX=exception.

seriesEventId.description: The ID for the recurring series master item, if this event is part of a recurring series (master too).

etag.description: The revision identifier that refers to the last modification.

createdAt.description: Creation timestamp in ISO 8601 format and UTC time.

updatedAt.description: Modification timestamp in ISO 8601 format and UTC time.

EventInstance.description: Represent an Event instance, a scheduled occurrence on a calendar, such as a meeting, holiday, or time block.

start.description: The (inclusive) start time of the event. For a recurring event, this is the start time of the first instance.

end.description: The (exclusive) end time of the event. For a recurring event, this is the end time of the first instance.

EventRecurrence.title: EventRecurrence
EventRecurrence.description: Defines for a recurring Event the repetition pattern and optionally the exceptions.

start.description: The (inclusive) start time of the recurrence.

rrule.description: The rule that define repeating pattern for recurring event instances.

exDates.description: Set of date/time exceptions of the recurrence.

EventAttendeeBase.title: EventAttendeeBase
EventAttendeeBase.description: Represent an Attendee object with base updateable fields.

userId.description: The Recipient's user ID, to bound this role to a specific User. Optional.

type.description: The type of attendee: IND=individual, RES=resource. Defaults to IND.

role.description: The role of attendee: CHA=chair, OPT=optional, REQ=required. Defaults to REQ.

EventAttendee.title: EventAttendee

responseStatus.description: The response type: NA=needsAction, DE=declined, TE=tentative, AC=accepted.

responseTimestamp.description: The date and time when the response was returned. It uses ISO 8601 format and UTC time.

EventAttendee.description: An event attendee that can be a person or resource such as a meeting room or equipment, that has been set up as a resource on the server configuration.

userId.description: The Organizer's user ID, to bound this role to a specific User. Optional.

iid.description: The event's unique ID that identifies an instance.

instanceType.description: The event instance type. The possible values are: SI=single, MA=master, OC=occurrence, EX=exception.

seriesEventId.description: The ID for the recurring series master item, if this event is part of a recurring series (master too).

etag.description: The revision identifier that refers to the last modification.

createdAt.description: Creation timestamp in ISO 8601 format and UTC time.

updatedAt.description: Modification timestamp in ISO 8601 format and UTC time.

EventChanged.title: EventChanged
EventChanged.description: Represent an intermediate object for better organizing changes.

EventResponse.title: EventResponse
EventResponse.description: Represent a base response object to an Event invitation.

comment.description: Text included in the response. Optional. (for future use)

notify.description: Specifies if a response is to be sent to the organizer. Default is true. For declined response the notification is always sent.

proposedNewTime.description: Only for tentative response. (for future use)

EventsResultDelta.title: EventsResultDelta
EventsResultDelta.description: Represent a response object that returns a collection of Events.

nextSyncToken.description: The syncToken that identifies the instant in past since to get changes.

EventsResult.title: EventsResult

totalCount.description: Items total count, usually used for pagination.

EventsResult.description: Represent a response object that returns a collection of Events.

OwnerInfo.title: OwnerInfo
OwnerInfo.description: Represent a user owning an item.

Recipient.title: Recipient
Recipient.description: The name and email address of a contact or message recipient.

address.description: The email address of the person or entity.

name.description: The display name of the person or entity.

auth-apikey-bearer.description: API-Key Authentication: specifies the secret token (bearer and username are both required)

auth-apikey-username.name: X-Auth-Username
auth-apikey-username.description: API-Key Authentication: specifies target username (bearer and username are both required)

_syncToken.name: _sync_token
_syncToken.description: Token exchanged between client and server that tracks changes from a precise state.

_select.name: _select
_select.description: List (comma-separated) of field names to include in resulting items. Optional, if omitted all available field will be taken into account.

_orderBy.name: _order_by
_orderBy.description: List (comma-separated) of field names and direction (ASC or DESC) to sort resulting items. Optional.

_pageNo.name: _page_no
_pageNo.description: The page number to return, providing a value actually activates pagination. Optional.

_pageSize.name: _page_size
_pageSize.description: How many items to return when paginating. Defaults to 50.

_returnCount.name: _return_count
_returnCount.description: Specifies whether to compute and return the full count of a list of items. Useful when dealing with paginated data. Optional.

_filter.name: _filter
_filter.description: A RSQL filter query to filter out resulting items. Optional.

_update.name: _update
_update.description: List (comma-separated) of field names to update. Optional, if omitted all available field will be taken into account.

modifySince.name: modify_since
modifySince.description: For recurring events specifies that the change affects instances from the current onward.

notify.name: notify
notify.description: Attendees who should receive notifications about the creation/modification of the new event.

eventUpdateOpts.name: update_options
eventUpdateOpts.description:
Bitmask that specifies which parts of the event must be updated. Multiple options can be combined by summing their values.

Flags: 1=Attendees

eventGetOpts.name: get_options
eventGetOpts.description:
Bitmask that specifies which parts of the event must be updated. Multiple options can be combined by summing their values.

Flags: 1=Attendees
